summary: Duplicate-driven matching pass produced 5 new function matches and 3 additional unmatched-item improvements across fighter, game-mode, item, and stage code. The PR body says the author reran `table-typer dups` after more functions had matched; the bot report records +2020 matched-code bytes and +0.05% overall matched code.

what_changed: Reran a duplicate-oriented matching workflow (`table-typer dups`) and applied resulting source-shape changes across 9 files.
Matched 5 functions according to bot report: gm_80163B9C, it_802703E8, ftCo_8009D81C, grAnime_801C84A4, and grKongo_801D55D8.
Improved 3 still-unmatched items according to bot report: it_802706D0, itLinkbomb_UnkMotion3_Anim, and it_802A4BFC.
In itcoll.c, rewrote a large item collision routine away from nested while/goto-like structure toward early return, for-loops over fighters/hits/hurts, and continue-based filtering.
In gm_1601.c, changed gm_80163B9C from returning after the first active entry to accumulating total frames across 0x19 entries and only producing output after the loop.
In granime.c, replaced duplicated inline logic for animation lookup with a call to grAnime_801C8318 and retained PAD_STACK(8).
In ftdynamics.c and grkongo.c, used PAD_STACK forms as narrow codegen tactics in matched functions.

smart_moves.text: Used duplicate detection as a focused search tactic after adjacent project progress had created more matched-function anchors.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Jelle suggested re-running `table-typer dups` now that more functions had matched
text_corpus: bot report lists 5 new matches and 3 improvements

smart_moves.text: Collapsed grAnime_801C84A4's expanded duplicate body into an existing helper call, reducing local source size while matching the function.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grAnime_801C84A4 replaced manual mask/setjmp/HSD_ForeachAnim sequence with grAnime_801C8318(gobj, arg1, arg2)

smart_moves.text: Converted a large collision routine to structured loops and early filters, which aligns with natural-loop cleanup and produced a reported function match.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: it_802703E8 uses for loops over HSD_GObj_Entities->fighters, hit_index, and hurt_index
text_corpus: bot report says it_802703E8 reached 100.00%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Duplicate matching can uncover real progress after more of the codebase has been matched; rerunning duplicate-finder tools later may expose newly actionable duplicates.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: reran `table-typer dups` after more functions matched
text_corpus: +2020 bytes, 5 new matches, 3 improvements

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a function duplicates a nearby helper, replacing expanded logic with the helper call can be the matching shape, but any required stack padding remains a tactic needing narrow evidence.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grAnime_801C84A4 calls grAnime_801C8318 and adds PAD_STACK(8)
text_corpus: grAnime_801C84A4 reached 100.00%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Loop and early-continue restructuring may match better than preserving decompiler-style nested control flow in collision code.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: it_802703E8 rewritten into for-loops and continues
text_corpus: it_802703E8 reached 100.00%

naming_conventions.name: hat
naming_conventions.lesson: Use a role name when the local points to a hat table entry; this replaced the generic `data` local in the matched fighter dynamics function.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: KirbyHatStruct* data -> KirbyHatStruct* hat

naming_conventions.name: aobj
naming_conventions.lesson: Short type-derived local names are acceptable when the role is simply the object being queried.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: HSD_AObj* aobj = grAnime_801C8318(...)

naming_conventions.name: temp_r31
naming_conventions.lesson: This PR used a register-style temp name for a small match-sensitive local; treat as match-specific evidence, not a general naming preference.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: qwe -> temp_r31

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Rerun `table-typer dups` after more functions match to find newly useful duplicate candidates.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: explicit `table-typer dups` rerun
text_corpus: bot report match/improvement table
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: The PR evidence supports this as a search tactic, not as proof that every duplicate suggestion is safe.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use PAD_STACK narrowly in functions where the reported result is a match.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftCo_8009D81C PAD_STACK(2 * 4)
diff_excerpt: grAnime_801C84A4 PAD_STACK(8)
diff_excerpt: grKongo_801D55D8 PAD_STACK(8)
text_corpus: these functions are listed as 100.00% after the PR
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Global standard says PAD_STACK needs explicit verifier evidence; here the bot report is evidence of resulting match but does not show adjacent regression checks.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Try helper substitution for duplicated expanded code.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grAnime_801C84A4 manual animation lookup collapsed into grAnime_801C8318 call
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Supported for this stage animation case only.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Change loop semantics to accumulate across all table entries when duplicate evidence points to that shape.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: gm_80163B9C total_frames += *gmMainLib_8015D6F8((u8) i) inside for i < 0x19
text_corpus: gm_80163B9C reached 100.00%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: This is function-specific; do not generalize the behavioral meaning beyond the shown code.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Rerun duplicate-finder tooling after more matched functions land
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2572 supports a workflow lesson: rerunning `table-typer dups` after more functions are matched can reveal modest but real matching opportunities across unrelated subsystems.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text
text_corpus: bot report with 5 new matches and 3 improvements

accepted_candidate_records.title: Duplicate-expanded helper bodies may collapse to existing helper calls
accepted_candidate_records.text: In grAnime_801C84A4, a large manual animation traversal block was replaced by grAnime_801C8318 plus PAD_STACK(8), and the bot report says the function reached 100.00%. Curate as a local example of checking for existing duplicate helpers before preserving expanded logic.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: grAnime_801C84A4
text_corpus: bot report

source_update_candidates.title: Document `table-typer dups` as a repeatable discovery tactic
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding a curator-reviewed note to workflow documentation: after significant matching progress, rerun duplicate-detection tooling such as `table-typer dups` to find newly actionable duplicate candidates. PR #2572 is one example, yielding 5 reported new matches and 3 improvements.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: author says rerunning `table-typer dups` was suggested
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a workflow/tooling note, not a code-standard fact; curator should decide where it belongs.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that Jelle reviewed or approved the PR; the slice only says Jelle suggested rerunning the tool.
Do not promote PAD_STACK as normal style from this PR; standards require narrow evidence, and the slice lacks adjacent regression details.
Do not treat temp_r31 as a preferred naming convention; it appears match-specific and conflicts with general conservative naming guidance unless needed for codegen.
Do not claim details of itlinkbomb.c or itlinkhookshot.c edits beyond changed-file metadata and bot improvement rows because their diffs are not loaded in the excerpt.
